title: Multiplying without barriers with Soroban and Suanpan (算盤)
header: Multiplying without barriers with Soroban and Suanpan (算盤)

text.text:
### Prerequisites

This game is a continuation of a previous game in which we performed the multiplication of a number with different orders (ones, tens, hundreds...) by another that has only ones (<htcite0>). Therefore, we suggest that you practice with this game before proceeding, in order to understand the steps of multiplication.

source.page: Practicing multiplication with Soroban and Suanpan (算盤)

text.text:
### Introduction

Our study of multiplication has been built step by step. In the previous step, we learned to multiply a number, regardless of the order it has (ones, tens, hundreds, etc.), by a single-digit number (<htcite0>).

However, as we will see in the following texts, there are situations in which we also need to multiply a large number by another. For this, we will need to use the concepts presented during the rereading again (<htcite1>), in order to carry out the new steps.

source.page: Practicing multiplication with Soroban and Suanpan (算盤)

text.text:
Reflection 1: Next step

We saw that, due to the rereading we did of numbers, we can start multiplication with the higher orders, always adding the results of the multiplications of the other orders ().

Now, all numbers in this text will have at least two orders (ones and tens). Given this, how would you use these orders to help with multiplication?

source.page: Practicing multiplication with Soroban and Suanpan (算盤)

text.text:
### Multiplying by large numbers

The general idea of how we should perform multiplication by larger numbers was already presented in the rereading text (<htcite0>). To make these steps clearer, we will once again use the abacuses developed in China (Zhongguo 中國) and Japan (Nippon koku or Nihon koku, 日本国).

To multiply a number by other numbers greater than 9, we will follow these steps:
- First, we will separate the number into its orders of magnitude. For example, 25 becomes 20 + 5.
- Next, we will multiply the number by the tens. In our example, we will multiply by 2 and, once we obtain the result, we will multiply that value by 10, shifting it one place to the left (or equivalently, adding a zero at the end).
- Then, we will multiply the number by the ones. In our example, by 5.
- Finally, we will add to the result of the multiplication by the ones the result obtained from the multiplication by the tens.

Do not worry if, for any reason, this description seems complex. Next, you will be able to perform this procedure in practice, with the app guiding each step of the process.
